mj-text-bKWuFGBO.content:
Hi {{contact.first_name}},

March brings a shift most investors miss:

Buyer sentiment strengthened in late 2025. Cap rates stabilized. Financing improved. And suddenly, multifamily investor confidence is rising again after two years of sitting on the sidelines.

Meanwhile, spring sellers are walking into the strongest seasonal advantage of the year: more buyers, faster sales, and stronger offers than any other season delivers.

Here's the disconnect: Most people think "market conditions are uncertain." The data shows momentum is building—and the window is now.

This month, we're unpacking two seasonal realities:

✅ Why multifamily investor confidence is returning—and what it means for deal flow
✅ Why spring sellers have the advantage—and how to leverage it
✅ March 2026 rate update: Where we stand as spring begins

Let's break it down.

mj-text-bKWuFGBO-piT1bAyZ.content: Spring Sellers Have an Edge. Here's Why.

mj-text-bKWuFGBO-piT1bAyZ-XkxBz27A.content: → Why Rising Buyer Demand, Stronger Offers, and Faster Sales Make This the Most Powerful Season to List Your Home

mj-text-zUU7TNhr.content:
Homeowners looking to sell want three things: buyers, strong offers, and speed.

Spring delivers all three.

1. More buyers are looking

Spring is peak moving season. ShowingTime data proves buyer activity peaks every year in spring.

This year? Mortgage rates are near 3-year lows.

More buyers + improving affordability = more eyes on your house.

Redfin confirms: "Homebuying demand is improving, and mortgage-purchase applications are near their highest level in three years."

More activity = more opportunity to close.

2. You'll get more offers

NAR data from the last three years shows sellers in spring consistently receive more offers than any other season.

Realtor.com: "Spring brings out buyers ready to move before summer. Listings see more views, showings, and offers."

That's good for your bottom line.

3. Homes sell 20 days faster

On average, homes sell 20 days faster in spring vs. winter. Almost three weeks shaved off your timeline.

Since homes have been taking longer to sell lately, listing during peak season sets you up to move quickly.

The reality:

Spring doesn't guarantee a sale. Strategy still matters.

But this season gives you momentum: more buyers, more activity, more opportunity.

If you're selling this year, why not do it when the odds are in your favor?

mj-text-iwAeSLUN.content: 🏠 Multifamily Investor Confidence Is Rising Again

mj-text-TwppHz2a.content: → Buyer Sentiment Strengthened in Late 2025 as Stable Cap Rates and Improving Financing Brought Investors Back

mj-text-C6AJmHxe.content:
For most of 2023-2024, multifamily investors stayed on the sidelines. Cap rates compressed. Financing costs doubled. Deal flow froze.

Late 2025 changed that.

Investor confidence started climbing. Not because rates dropped dramatically—but because uncertainty cleared.

What shifted:

Cap rates found a floor – After two years of repricing, cap rates landed at 5.5-6.5% and held. Pricing clarity emerged. Deal flow returned.

Financing became accessible – Agency debt stabilized. HUD/FHA multifamily rates dropped to 5.18-5.67%—the lowest in years. Deals penciled again.

Institutional capital re-entered – After sitting on billions, institutional investors started deploying in Q4 2025. Private investors followed.

What this means for 2026:

Confidence is rising. Deal flow is accelerating. Capital is moving.

Winners are executing on:

  • Value-add properties with operational upside

  • Secondary markets with population growth

  • Stabilized assets in supply-constrained areas

They're not betting on appreciation. They're betting on cash flow at current rates.

The strategic advantage:

Early 2026 is the window before everyone else catches on.

Investor confidence is rising. But it hasn't peaked yet.

That gap—between rising confidence and peak competition—is your opportunity.

Spring won't have less competition. Q2 won't have better pricing.

The move is now.

mj-text-DKUspLNS.content: 💰March 2026 Interest Rate Snapshot

mj-text-dcG0v2OP.content:
Here’s where financing stands this month for both multifamily and single-family deals.

Sources: Residential rates sourced from Freddie Mac, CBS News, Bankrate, Fortune, Zillow as of March 12-19, 2026. Commercial rates sourced from Commercial Loan Direct, Apartment Loan Store, Select Commercial as of March 2-17, 2026.

mj-text-Lvtk2ipD.content: 🔑 Key Takeaway: Market Wisdom for March 2026

mj-text-sr3BwGDZ.content:
If you're selling single-family:

Spring is your power season. More buyers. More offers. Faster sales.

List now. Don't wait until inventory floods.

If you're investing in multifamily:

Investor confidence is rising. Deal flow is back.

The window is early 2026—before confidence turns into competition.

mj-text-eY3Yqusd.content: Ready to Stop Waiting and Start Building?

mj-text-3rQjpfCg.content:
Spring Doesn't Wait

April brings more listings. May brings more competition.

March still has breathing room.

Most people figure this out in May. You're reading this in March.

Act accordingly.

We're working with clients right now on spring positioning.

Sellers: timing your listing for maximum impact.

Investors: identifying deals before pricing adjusts.

Brian Norton
Principal, Olive Tree Investments
📧 brian@olivetreeinv.io | 📞 404-643-2356
📍 269 Market Place Blvd Suite 126, Cartersville, GA 30121
